repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue Stat entry studies this NPIV projection-functional Gaussian sequence modulus; closest repo entries stat_ate_overlap_decay and stat_policy_regret_margin_overlap have unrelated causal targets and frontiers.

published_prior_art.cite: BreunigJohannes2009LocalNPIR derives minimax rates for NPIR linear functionals under structural-function and representer ellipsoids (https://arxiv.org/abs/0902.2103); CaiLow2005AdaptationConfidenceIntervals gives the generic modulus machinery (https://arxiv.org/abs/math/0503662). Under ass:projection-domain, h^Pi is just an exact solution to T h = g^R, so the residual no-solution component is absent from the sequence experiment.

repo_prior_art.cite: No repo proposal records the realized-functional versus representer-envelope bookkeeping for no-solution NPIV.

published_prior_art.cite: BreunigJohannes2009LocalNPIR already indexes NPIR linear-functional rates by ellipsoids for the structural function and functional representer (https://arxiv.org/abs/0902.2103); taking the supremum over Psi(b,M) is the standard exact-solution envelope once g^R is substituted for g.

repo_prior_art.cite: No repo entry records this diagonalized Riesz-norm sanity check for the proposed target.

published_prior_art.cite: BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2022StronglyIdentifiedWeaklyIdentified characterizes sqrt(n)-regular linear functionals by a dual/debiasing representer condition (https://arxiv.org/abs/2208.08291); the proposal itself presents this as a recovered equivalence.

repo_prior_art.cite: No repo entry has polynomial/exponential NPIV projection-functional phase formulas.

published_prior_art.cite: BreunigJohannes2009LocalNPIR gives the linear-functional ellipsoid algebra (https://arxiv.org/abs/0902.2103), while ChenReiss2011RateOptimalityIllPosedEconometrics and HallHorowitz2006NonparametricMethodsIV give the mild/severe ill-posed NPIV rate architecture (https://arxiv.org/abs/0709.2003; https://arxiv.org/abs/math/0603130). The displayed profile rates are this known algebra after replacing the exact reduced form by g^R.

repo_prior_art.cite: No repo Stat entry records this sanity reduction for this qid.

published_prior_art.cite: BreunigJohannes2009LocalNPIR and BreunigJohannes2011AdaptiveFunctionalsNPIR are exactly the exact-solution NPIR benchmarks to which this proposition reduces (https://arxiv.org/abs/0902.2103; https://arxiv.org/abs/1109.0961).

repo_prior_art.cite: No repo proposal gives an ordered-Lepski adaptive result for this no-solution NPIV projection target.

published_prior_art.cite: BreunigJohannes2011AdaptiveFunctionalsNPIR already gives data-driven dimension selection/Lepski adaptation for exact-solution NPIR linear functionals up to logarithmic loss (https://arxiv.org/abs/1109.0961), and CaiLow2006AdaptiveLinearFunctionals gives ordered-modulus adaptive estimators (https://arxiv.org/abs/math/0602299). With ass:projection-domain, the proposed no-solution version is the same sequence problem for h^Pi.

novelty_flags.label: headline modulus and adaptive kernel
novelty_flags.one_line: After ass:projection-domain, h^Pi solves the projected exact inverse problem T h = g^R, so the Gaussian sequence modulus, envelope, profile rates, Riesz boundary, and Lepski adaptation are subsumed by the exact-solution NPIR/linear-functional literature rather than being a new no-solution frontier.

novelty_flags.label: comparator_promise_table / Shen and Breunig-Johannes rows
novelty_flags.one_line: The table advertises strict tightening of Shen et al. and Breunig-Johannes adaptive work, but no statement is formulated as a strict rate comparison against either paper's published estimator/rate; the formal results are generic exact-solution sequence claims for g^R.

soundness_flags.label: conj:adaptive-ordered-modulus
soundness_flags.one_line: The adaptive conjecture uses 'severe', 'regular mildly ill-posed', and 'irregular mildly ill-posed' as regimes without spelling the alpha,beta,tau partition, and reuses C_ad as both selector threshold and risk constant.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:gaussian-sequence / all rate statements
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The review accepts the singular-coordinate Gaussian model as a reduced experiment, not as a full i.i.d. NPIV theorem.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: All minimax and adaptive claims are conditional Gaussian-sequence claims until an asymptotic-equivalence or embedding theorem is added.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:projection-domain / h^Pi
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The review accepts g^R in range(T) as the Shen-style least-squares nonemptiness/domain condition, but it makes h^Pi an exact solution for the projected reduced form.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The headline is bound to the projected exact equation T h^Pi = g^R and does not characterize extra difficulty created by the orthogonal no-solution residual r.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Pivot the headline to a full i.i.d. no-solution NPIV theorem where estimating T, g^R, and the projection residual creates a term not present in exact-solution NPIR; otherwise demote the current modulus to a sanity lemma.

If the intended novelty is a comparator result against Shen et al., state Shen's published Tikhonov rate with an equation/assumption anchor and prove an explicit strict inequality or equivalence frontier against R_n^*(psi).

For the adaptive layer, replace the current conjecture with a between-class modulus lower bound and an ordered-Lepski upper theorem whose regime partition is written explicitly in alpha,beta,tau and whose log-loss comparison is not inherited verbatim from Breunig-Johannes/Cai-Low.

recommended_next_step: Pivot or substantially reframe before Stage 0: the current kernel is a published exact-solution sequence theorem applied to g^R, below novelty_target=field.
